test_cases.description: Non-profit telehealth hub opening — philanthropy-funded, two spokespeople, no embargo
test_cases.input_summary: Meridian Health Network opens $12M telehealth hub in Phoenix serving 400,000 rural patients; quotes from CMO and foundation director; no embargo
test_cases.notes: Textbook inverted-pyramid structure. Headline states news value clearly. Opening paragraph answers all five Ws. Quotes placed correctly after first paragraph. Wire-ready formatting including dateline, boilerplate, contact block, and end mark.

test_cases.description: Tech company executive appointment — single spokesperson, no second quote, embargo 7 days
test_cases.input_summary: Harlow Systems appoints Priya Nair as Chief Technology Officer; previously VP Engineering at a fintech; quote from CEO about strategic hire; embargo until March 23
test_cases.notes: Solid structure and correct embargo formatting. Relevance deduction: output included a generic paragraph about the company's growth trajectory not grounded in the input. Claude flagged the detail as unconfirmed, which is correct behaviour, but the paragraph still appeared in the draft — slight actionability cost. Consistency note: all provided facts reproduced accurately.

test_cases.description: Industry award win — single spokesperson, data point, trade publication target
test_cases.input_summary: Castlebrook Architecture wins Building Industry Association award for sustainable design on a public library project; 40% reduction in energy use; quote from founding partner; targeting trade press
test_cases.notes: Strong trade-register calibration. Technical vocabulary appropriate for architecture press. Coherence slightly lower: transitional sentence between award context and methodology paragraph was awkward. One sentence in body ran to 45 words — borderline for wire style. All facts consistent with input.
